sections.context: Burrow V1 is the OS-isolated sandbox runtime layer for the os-eco coding-agent ecosystem (greenhouse → overstory → sapling/claude-code). Today, agents either run on the host (polluting toolchains, risky filesystem access) or inside Docker (heavyweight, slow). Burrow wraps native OS sandbox primitives — bwrap on Linux, sandbox-exec on macOS — so any CLI-based coding agent runs in a workspace-scoped jail with declared network policy and explicit env passthrough. V1 is local-first and single-user; the BurrowProvider and AgentRuntime seams are designed so remote substrates (fly/AWS) and new agents land as additions, not rewrites. This epic decomposes the work per SPEC.md §22.
sections.approach: Implement V1 in 11 sequential phases (0-10) per SPEC.md §22, totaling ~3-4k LOC over 12-16 focused days. Each phase produces a working slice end-to-end against its scope so we can verify SPEC.md §23 success criteria incrementally. Architecture is Bun + TypeScript strict, citty CLI on top of a typed Client library, drizzle-on-bun:sqlite for durable state, p-queue for per-burrow concurrency. Native sandbox wrappers (no srt, no Docker) so Burrow owns the contract. Two seams kept first-class: BurrowProvider (LocalProvider in V1, FlyProvider stub) and AgentRuntime (claude-code, sapling, codex built-ins plus declarative AgentConfig). Each phase becomes a child seed that can be sd plan submit'd individually when picked up — this top-level plan deliberately stops at phase granularity to avoid freezing detail before we learn from earlier phases.

alternatives.name: Use Anthropic's srt (sandbox-runtime) wrapper instead of writing native bwrap/sandbox-exec
alternatives.rejected_because: Adds a third-party CLI dependency in the critical path of daily workflow and gives up ownership of the profile contract. Future provider implementations (Fly, AWS) need to generate equivalent profiles directly anyway. Native primitives are stable OS interfaces; ~500-800 LOC that changes rarely once correct (SPEC.md §8.6).

alternatives.name: Docker-based isolation
alternatives.rejected_because: OS-level sandbox primitives are dramatically lighter than containers, with no image management, no daemon, no per-burrow pull. Burrow is explicitly not a container manager (SPEC.md §2.3).

alternatives.name: Build remote provider (Fly) in V1 alongside LocalProvider
alternatives.rejected_because: Doubles scope. The BurrowProvider seam earns its keep regardless; shipping the second implementation can be additive post-V1 (SPEC.md §3.2).

alternatives.name: Postgres + Redis + HTTP server from day one
alternatives.rejected_because: V1 is local-first and single-user — SQLite WAL handles durable state in-process, no daemon, no external services. Reversibility for future server modes is preserved by drizzle's multi-dialect support (SPEC.md §6).

alternatives.name: Big-bang implementation rather than 11 phases
alternatives.rejected_because: Each phase produces a verifiable slice tied to SPEC.md §23 success criteria. Phasing also means quality gates run continuously and we discover spec gaps early.

steps.title: Phase 0 — Scaffold the Bun/TypeScript project skeleton
Phase 1 — Native sandbox wrappers (bwrap + sandbox-exec) with SSH passthrough and network policy
Phase 2 — SQLite data model, drizzle schema, repos, and per-burrow run loop with crash recovery
Phase 3 — Workspace materialization: git worktree for project burrows, fork for task burrows
Phase 4 — AgentRuntime interface + built-ins (claude-code, sapling, codex) + declarative AgentConfig
Phase 5 — Inbox + steering: messages table, send/chat commands, next-turn injection
Phase 6 — Event tail + archive: in-memory pub/sub, logs/events --follow, NDJSON archiver on destroy
Phase 7 — Library API + full CLI surface: Client class, all commands wired, JSON/pretty modes, branding pass
Phase 8 — burrow.toml schema, secrets resolution, toolchain doctor (with op:// 1Password support)
Phase 9 — burrow ship: build + deploy artifacts with fly and render as first ShipTargets
Phase 10 — Polish: README, examples, error message review, branding compliance, shell completions

risks: bwrap and sandbox-exec have subtly different capability models; profile drift between Linux and macOS could let one platform succeed where the other silently leaks. Mitigation: shared SandboxProfile contract validated by integration tests on both platforms.

git worktree against the host clone gives the agent read-write access to .git/, so a misbehaving agent could corrupt host git metadata. Mitigation: read_only_main_branch flag plus path-policy constraints (SPEC.md §11.2).

Spawn-per-turn vs one-shot runtimes diverge enough that the inbox-injection abstraction may leak. Mitigation: encodeInboxMessage hook on the runtime + clear CLI surfacing when targeting a one-shot runtime (SPEC.md §13.3).

Network policy enforcement on Linux: userspace HTTP proxy vs nftables. The latter is more correct but less portable. Decision deferred per open question §25.3 — ship userspace proxy first, nftables opt-in later.

Drizzle is the only abstraction layered on bun:sqlite. If drizzle's bun:sqlite adapter regresses, we may hit blockers. Mitigation: keep schema and queries simple enough that we could drop to raw bun:sqlite if needed.

Toolchain mounting strategy (specific binary vs full $PATH ancestry) interacts badly with shim-based managers (mise, asdf, fnm). Decision deferred per open question §25.4 — V1 mounts resolved binary + lib dir, shim-aware mode via burrow.toml flag.

Claude Code credential cache forwarding is convenience-critical but couples us to Anthropic's auth layout. Mitigation: detect-and-skip-on-failure rather than hard requirement; user can always log in inside burrow.
acceptance: burrow up in a project with a burrow.toml succeeds and agents run inside an isolated sandbox with the host filesystem outside the workspace unreachable (SPEC.md §23).
Four parallel burrows (one project + three task burrows) can run agents simultaneously without races.
kill -9 on the burrow process leaves the system recoverable: in-flight runs marked failed, burrows still listed, workspaces intact.
burrow send <id> "<msg>" is delivered on the next turn for claude-code and sapling runtimes.
burrow events --follow interleaves events from all active burrows in real-time as NDJSON.
burrow destroy <id> archives the burrow's complete event log to ${dataDir}/archive/<id>/events.jsonl.
Adding a Gemini-CLI-shaped agent requires only a [[agents]] entry in burrow.toml — zero code changes in src/.
burrow doctor correctly identifies a missing host toolchain declared in burrow.toml and refuses to up until fixed.
A new IC running `bun install -g @os-eco/burrow-cli && cd project && burrow up` is productive without further setup.

A future FlyProvider can be added without modifying any file under src/core/, src/db/, src/runtime/, src/inbox/, src/events/, or src/runner/ — the seam holds (the load-bearing test of the architecture).
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck pass cleanly across the whole codebase at the end of every phase.
